title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe | Authentic Indian
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken! Get our authentic Indian recipe for juicy, smoky chicken perfect for dinner. Easy step-by-step guide.

easy Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken recipe
authentic Indian Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken
Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken step by step
best Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home
seo.h1: Authentic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe

seo.imageAlt: Platter of vibrant red charcoal grilled tandoori chicken with lemon wedges and cilantro
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky, spicy flavors of authentic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, perfect for your next Indian dinner.

description: Experience the fiery, smoky allure of authentic Tandoori Chicken, expertly charcoal-grilled to perfection. Each succulent piece is marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, promising a burst of traditional Indian flavors with a delightful char. This recipe brings the taste of Indian restaurants right to your backyard grill.

ingredients.notes: cut into 8-10 pieces, skin removed, deep slits made

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken for First Marinade
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry. Make deep gashes on each piece to allow the marinade to penetrate. In a large bowl, combine 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, and 1 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the thick yogurt, remaining lemon juice, remaining ginger-garlic paste, remaining salt, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, tandoori masala powder, mustard oil, and red food color (if using). Mix until smooth and well combined.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken from the first marinade to the second marinade. Ensure every piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor and tenderness. The longer the marination, the better the result.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: Arrange charcoal in your grill for two-zone cooking: a hot side for direct heat and a cooler side for indirect heat. Light the charcoal and let it burn until covered in a light gray ash. This usually takes about 20-30 minutes. Clean and oil the grill grates well to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces on the hot side of the grill over direct heat. Sear each side for 2-3 minutes to get nice char marks. Then, move the chicken to the cooler, indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and cook for about 20-25 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Baste and Finish Grilling
instructions.text: During the last 10 minutes of cooking, baste the chicken pieces with melted butter or ghee. Continue grilling, turning frequently, until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and has a beautiful charred exterior. An instant-read thermometer should register 165F (74C) in the thickest part of the chicken.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the Tandoori chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with lemon wedges, mint chutney, or sliced onions.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, ensure you use full-fat, thick yogurt. Hanging regular yogurt in a muslin cloth for an hour can achieve this consistency.
Do not skip the deep slits on the chicken; they are crucial for the marinade to penetrate deeply, ensuring every bite is flavorful and moist.
Always allow for a long marination period, ideally overnight. This not only infuses flavor but also tenderizes the chicken significantly.
article:
Unleash the captivating aromas and vibrant flavors of India right in your backyard with a dish that has captivated palates worldwide: Tandoori Chicken. While traditionally cooked in a scorching hot clay tandoor oven, we’re bringing that irresistible smoky char to your home grill. Get ready to master the art of making authentic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, a show-stopping Indian dinner recipe that promises juicy, flavorful chicken with that unmistakable smoky kiss. Forget dry, bland chicken; this recipe guarantees a culinary experience that’s rich in tradition and bursting with taste.

## What is Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken?

At its heart, Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish featuring chicken marinated in yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooked in a cylindrical clay oven known as a tandoor. The high heat of the tandoor quickly cooks the chicken, sealing in juices while creating a beautifully charred exterior. When we talk about Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, we're replicating this magic using the open flame and intense heat of a charcoal grill. The charcoal imparts a deep, smoky flavor that complements the tangy, spicy marinade perfectly, giving the chicken a depth of flavor that's hard to achieve with other cooking methods. This grilling technique ensures the chicken remains incredibly moist and tender on the inside, with a tantalizingly crisp and slightly charred skin on the outside. It's a staple Indian dinner recipe, loved for its robust flavors and satisfying texture, making it a perfect centerpiece for any gathering.

## Ingredients You Need for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Crafting the perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken begins with selecting the right ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need to gather:

*   **Chicken:** 2-3 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, or a whole cut-up chicken work best). The bone and skin add incredible flavor and moisture during grilling.
*   **Full-Fat Yogurt:** 1 cup. Greek yogurt or hung curd is ideal as its thickness prevents the marinade from becoming too watery.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 2 tablespoons. Freshly made is always superior.
*   **Lemon Juice:** 2 tablespoons. Adds tang and helps tenderize the chicken.
*   **Mustard Oil:** 2 tablespoons (optional, but highly recommended for authentic flavor) or a neutral oil like vegetable or canola oil.
*   **Tandoori Masala Powder:** 2 tablespoons. This pre-mixed spice blend is key to the distinctive tandoori flavor.
*   **Kashmiri Red Chili Powder:** 1 tablespoon. Provides a vibrant red color without excessive heat. You can substitute with regular red chili powder for more spice, or paprika for color with less heat.
*   **Turmeric Powder:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Cumin Powder:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Coriander Powder:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Garam Masala:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Red Food Coloring (optional):** A few drops, for that iconic bright red hue. Alternatively, rely on Kashmiri chili powder for natural color.
*   **For Garnish:** Fresh cilantro, chopped; lemon wedges.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Follow these steps to create succulent Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make several deep slits (about 1/2 inch deep) into the thickest parts of the chicken. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply, ensuring maximum flavor.
2.  **First Marinade (Optional but Recommended):** In a bowl, combine 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub this mixture all over the chicken pieces, making sure to get it into the slits. Let it sit for 15-20 minutes. This initial step helps tenderize the chicken and adds a foundational layer of flavor.
3.  **Prepare the Main Marinade:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the full-fat yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, remaining lemon juice, mustard oil (or neutral oil), tandoori masala, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, and salt. If using, add a few drops of red food coloring and mix well until a smooth, vibrant paste forms.
4.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the chicken pieces to the marinade, ensuring each piece is thoroughly coated, working the marinade into the slits. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight (12-24 hours) for the best results. The longer the marination, the more flavorful and tender your chicken will be.
5.  **Prepare Your Charcoal Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the coals to create two heat zones: one direct heat zone (coals directly under the grate) for searing and charring, and one indirect heat zone (no coals directly under the grate) for slower cooking. Aim for a medium-high heat (about 375-400°F or 190-200°C). Clean the grill grates thoroughly and lightly oil them to prevent sticking.
6.  **Grill the Chicken:** Remove the chicken from the marinade, letting any excess drip off. Do not wipe off the marinade. Place the chicken pieces over the direct heat zone of the grill. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until you get a nice char and grill marks.
7.  **Cook Through:** Once charred, move the chicken to the indirect heat zone. Close the grill lid (if applicable) and continue to cook for 25-35 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) using a meat thermometer. Baste with any leftover marinade or a little oil during the last 10 minutes of cooking to keep it moist and enhance flavor.
8.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, transfer the Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken to a platter, cover loosely with foil, and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness. Garnish with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken Results

*   **Quality Chicken Matters:** Always opt for bone-in, skin-on chicken. The bone helps conduct heat evenly, and the skin protects the meat from drying out while adding flavor.
*   **Deep Slits are Key:** Don't be shy with the knife! Deeper slits allow the marinade to penetrate more effectively, leading to more flavorful and tender chicken.
*   **Marination Time is Non-Negotiable:** For true Tandoori flavor and tenderness, marinate for a minimum of 6 hours, and ideally 12-24 hours. The yogurt's lactic acid tenderizes the meat, and the spices infuse deeply.
*   **Thick Yogurt:** Use full-fat Greek yogurt or hung curd. If your yogurt is too thin, drain it in a cheesecloth for an hour or two to remove excess water. This prevents the marinade from sliding off the chicken.
*   **Embrace Mustard Oil:** While optional, mustard oil adds a pungent, authentic flavor that is characteristic of North Indian cuisine. If you don't have it, a neutral oil works, but the flavor profile will be slightly different.
*   **Manage Grill Temperature:** Control flare-ups by keeping a spray bottle of water handy. If the chicken starts to burn, move it to the indirect heat zone. Consistent medium-high heat is crucial for that perfect char and even cooking.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and can lead to steaming rather than grilling, preventing a good char.
*   **Rest the Chicken:** Just like any grilled meat, allowing the Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken to rest after cooking ensures the juices redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a much juicier final product.

## Serving Suggestions for Tandoori Chicken

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a magnificent dish on its own, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments. Here are some serving suggestions to complete your Indian dinner recipe experience:

*   **Naan or Roti:** Warm, soft naan bread or whole wheat roti are perfect for scooping up the chicken and its delicious juices.
*   **Mint-Cilantro Chutney:** A refreshing, tangy green chutney made with mint, cilantro, green chilies, and yogurt or lemon juice is an absolute must-have. Its coolness beautifully contrasts the spicy chicken.
*   **Onion Rings and Lemon Wedges:** Simple, raw red onion rings and fresh lemon wedges add a crucial burst of acidity and sharpness that cuts through the richness of the chicken.
*   **Kachumber Salad:** A light, refreshing salad made with finely diced cucumber, tomatoes, onions, and a lemon-cumin dressing provides a textural and flavor contrast.
*   **Jeera Rice or Pulao:** A side of fragrant basmati rice, either plain or lightly spiced with cumin (Jeera Rice) or a vegetable Pulao, makes for a more substantial meal.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based dip, often with cucumber or boondi, helps balance the heat of the Tandoori Chicken.

## Variations and Substitutions for Tandoori Chicken

While the classic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a timeless favorite, don't hesitate to experiment with variations and substitutions to suit your taste and dietary preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Options:** The tandoori marinade is incredibly versatile. Substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), large mushrooms, cauliflower florets (gobi), or even firm tofu for delicious vegetarian tandoori dishes. Marinate and grill them similarly, adjusting cooking times.
*   **Other Meats:** Lamb chops, fish fillets (especially firm white fish), or large shrimp can also be marinated and grilled tandoori style. Adjust marination and cooking times accordingly. Fish and shrimp will require much shorter marination and cooking times.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to control the heat. For a milder version, reduce or omit chili powder and rely more on Kashmiri chili powder for color. For extra spice, add finely chopped green chilies to the marinade.
*   **Natural Coloring:** If you prefer to avoid food coloring, increase the amount of Kashmiri red chili powder for a natural, albeit slightly less vibrant, red hue. A small amount of beet juice can also impart a natural red color.
*   **Grill Alternatives:** While charcoal grilling provides the best flavor, you can also bake Tandoori Chicken in an oven (at 400°F/200°C), broil it, or even cook it in an air fryer. However, these methods won't replicate the smoky flavor and char that only a charcoal grill can provide. For a hint of smoke without a charcoal grill, you can use liquid smoke in the marinade or finish under the broiler after oven baking.

Embrace the sizzle, the smoke, and the incredible flavors as you prepare this iconic Indian dinner recipe. Making Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home is a rewarding culinary adventure that brings a taste of India's rich culinary heritage directly to your table, promising a memorable meal for all who gather to enjoy it.
